commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds browser doctor/facade/health-check detection, safe `doctor --fix` archival, docs, and tests for stale legacy `browser/clawd` profile residue.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main resolves the canonical managed browser profile to `openclaw` but has no detector or archive path for leftover `browser/clawd` profile data, while the linked issue provides the filesystem shape.
commands.review_followup: A narrow docs sync finding is safe for an automated repair lane; the protected label still requires maintainer review before merge.

target.title: fix(doctor): archive legacy clawd browser profile residue
target.body:
Fixes #45404.

## Summary

- Detect legacy `~/.openclaw/browser/clawd` managed-browser profile residue from the browser doctor path when the canonical `openclaw` managed profile is active.
- Add an explicit `doctor --fix` archival path through the browser facade and repair sequence, using the browser plugin's safe trash mover rather than deleting in place.
- Skip both warning and archival when `clawd` is still configured as a browser profile or referenced by an explicit `userDataDir`.

## Real behavior proof

![PR 83230 before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83230-fresh/pr-83230/pr-83230-fix-doctor-archive-legacy-clawd-browser-profile--before-after-proof.png)

Fresh deterministic proof artifacts:
- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83230-fresh/pr-83230/summary.txt
- Before/source proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83230-fresh/pr-83230/before-source-proof.log
- After/PR proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83230-fresh/pr-83230/after-pr-83230.log

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Rename-era installs can leave stale `browser/clawd/user-data` profile data on disk even though current managed browser launch uses `browser/openclaw/user-data`; before this patch, browser doctor did not surface or repair that residue.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu 24.04 under WSL, running the production browser doctor facade and repair sequence against filesystem-backed path conditions for `~/.openclaw/browser/clawd/user-data` and the canonical `~/.openclaw/browser/openclaw/user-data` path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `openclaw doctor`/`openclaw doctor --fix` equivalent production paths through the browser doctor facade and repair sequence with a stale legacy profile path present, then ran the focused commands below:

```text
CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts src/commands/doctor-browser.facade.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts
CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.internal.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/profiles-service.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server.agent-contract-core.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/browser/browser-doctor.ts extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.ts extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts src/commands/doctor-browser.ts src/commands/doctor-browser.facade.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:changed
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the after-fix browser doctor path includes the visible cleanup guidance and the after-fix repair path archives the stale directory:

```text
Browser
- Legacy managed browser profile residue was found at /tmp/openclaw-home/browser/clawd.
- The canonical OpenClaw-managed browser profile is /tmp/openclaw-home/browser/openclaw/user-data.
- If no browser is using the legacy profile, run openclaw doctor --fix to archive it safely instead of deleting it in place.

Doctor changes
Archived legacy clawd managed browser profile residue.
- legacy profile: /tmp/openclaw-home/browser/clawd
- canonical profile: /tmp/openclaw-home/browser/openclaw/user-data
- archived at: /tmp/openclaw-home/browser/.trash/clawd
```

The configured-profile safety path also produced no cleanup call when `browser.defaultProfile` or `browser.profiles.clawd` still referenced `clawd`.

**Observed result after fix:** The browser doctor surfaces stale legacy `clawd` residue, the explicit repair lane archives it safely, and configured/active `clawd` profiles remain untouched.

**What was not tested:** No migrated macOS/Brave machine with real January rename-era profile residue was available; the proof uses the production browser doctor and repair functions with deterministic local filesystem path conditions instead of a live historical profile.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
